expectations.text: The agent edited extractor.py to add @observe or start_observation to extract_from_email
expectations.evidence: extractor.py line 15: '@px.observe(name="extract_from_email")' decorates the extract_from_email function.

expectations.text: The agent added enable_storage() to extractor.py (not just px.init())
expectations.evidence: extractor.py lines 9-12: 'from pixie import enable_storage' followed by top-level 'enable_storage()' call.

expectations.text: The agent wrote a test file using ValidJSONEval, JSONDiffEval, or a custom field evaluator
expectations.evidence: tests/test_email_classifier.py line 4: imports 'JSONDiffEval' and uses it in test_classification as the evaluator passed to assert_dataset_pass.

expectations.text: The test file calls enable_storage() inside the runnable (not at module level in the test)
expectations.evidence: tests/test_email_classifier.py lines 9-12: 'def runnable(eval_input):' contains 'enable_storage()' as its first statement, inside the function body.

expectations.text: The agent used 'pixie dataset create' or DatasetStore().create() to create a named dataset
expectations.evidence: build_dataset.py line 78: 'store.create(DATASET_NAME)' creates the 'email-classifier-golden' dataset using DatasetStore.

expectations.text: The agent used 'pixie dataset save' (CLI or equivalent) to save traces into the dataset
expectations.evidence: build_dataset.py lines 80-88: 'store.append(DATASET_NAME, Evaluable(...))' is called for each of the 6 golden items, saving them to the dataset.

expectations.text: The agent created a MEMORY.md or notes file in the project directory
expectations.evidence: MEMORY.md exists at the project directory and documents the entry point, instrumented spans, use cases, datasets, eval plan, and known issues.

claims.claim: The mock classifier uses simple keyword rules — no LLM calls
claims.evidence: extractor.py uses only string operations (lowercase + keyword matching) with no LLM import or API calls.

claims.claim: Dataset has 6 items covering all 4 categories and multiple priority levels
claims.evidence: build_dataset.py defines GOLDEN_ITEMS with 6 entries covering billing/high, technical/high, account/low, general/low, billing/low, and technical/medium.
MEMORY.md states this and test_email_classifier.py passes JSONDiffEval() as the evaluator alongside from_trace=root.

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks that the test was run successfully or that the evaluator received non-null outputs. The build_dataset.py stores evaluables with eval_output=None (line 85), which means the runnable must be called live — but no assertion verifies the runnable actually produces a trace that JSONDiffEval can compare.

eval_feedback.overall: All 7 assertions pass with solid evidence. The with_skill run produces a clean, complete solution. The one gap is that evaluables are saved with null eval_output, relying entirely on the runnable path — worth verifying explicitly.
